value.found_quote: In your Codex home directory (defaults to `~/.codex`, unless you set `CODEX_HOME`), Codex reads `AGENTS.override.md` if it exists. Otherwise, Codex reads `AGENTS.md`. Codex uses only the first non-empty file at this level.
value.rationale: The page describes `AGENTS.override.md` files taking precedence over `AGENTS.md` at both the global scope (Codex home directory checks override first) and project scope (each directory checks `AGENTS.override.md`, then `AGENTS.md`). This substantiates the claim. The page presents it as a current capability with no deprecation notice.
